# terrible_cisco_aci_aci_tenant_action_rule_profile (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `action_rule` (String) The name of the action rule profile.
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `description` (String) The description for the action rule profile.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `multipath` (Boolean) Set action rule based on set redistribute multipath configuration. Can not be configured along with `set_route_tag`. Can not be configured for APIC version 4.2 and prior. The APIC defaults to `false` when unset.
- `name_alias` (String) The alias for the current object. This relates to the nameAlias field in ACI.
- `next_hop_propagation` (Boolean) The set action rule based on nexthop unchanged configuration. Can not be configured along with `set_route_tag`. Can not be configured for APIC version 4.2 and prior. The APIC defaults to `false` when unset.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `set_as_path` (String) List of Autonomous System (AS) path prepend configurations. Providing an empty list `set_as_path=[]` will remove all AS path configurations. This is only supported in Cisco ACI Release 6.0(2) and higher.
- `set_communities` (String) List of additional communities to add to the action rule profile using the append criteria. Providing an empty list `set_communities=[]` will remove all communities. This is only supported in Cisco ACI Release 6.0(2) and higher.
- `set_community` (String) The set action rule based on communities. To delete this attribute, pass an empty dictionary.
- `set_dampening` (String) The set action rule based on dampening. To delete this attribute, pass an empty dictionary.
- `set_metric` (String) The set action rule based on metric. To delete this attribute, pass an empty string.
- `set_metric_type` (String) The set action rule based on a metric type. To delete this attribute, pass an empty string.
- `set_next_hop` (String) The set action rule based on the next hop address. To delete this attribute, pass an empty string.
- `set_policy_tag` (String) The set action rule based on policy tag (External EPG or ESG). Providing an empty dictionary `set_policy_tag={}` will remove this attribute. Either External EPG (l3out and external_epg) or ESG (ap and esg) must be configured, but not both. This is only supported in Cisco ACI Release 6.0(2) and higher.
- `set_preference` (String) The set action rule based on preference. To delete this attribute, pass an empty string.
- `set_route_tag` (String) The set action rule based on route tag. Can not be configured along with `next_hop_propagation` and `multipath`. To delete this attribute, pass an empty string.
- `set_weight` (String) The set action rule based on weight. To delete this attribute, pass an empty string.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) Use `present` or `absent` for adding or removing. Use `query` for listing an object or multiple objects.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `tenant` (String) The name of the tenant.
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `current` (String) The existing configuration from the APIC after the module has finished
- `error` (String) The error information as returned from the APIC
- `filter_string` (String) The filter string used for the request
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
- `method` (String) The HTTP method used for the request to the APIC
- `previous` (String) The original configuration from the APIC before the module has started
- `proposed` (String) The assembled configuration from the user-provided parameters
- `raw` (String) The raw output returned by the APIC REST API (xml or json)
- `response` (String) The HTTP response from the APIC
- `sent` (String) The actual/minimal configuration pushed to the APIC
- `status` (Number) The HTTP status from the APIC
- `url` (String) The HTTP url used for the request to the APIC
